content: Diagnosis requires: (1) Evidence of hepatic steatosis - imaging (ultrasound first-line, shows increased echogenicity; CAP on FibroScan; MRI-PDFF most accurate) or histology; (2) Exclusion of significant alcohol use; (3) Exclusion of other liver diseases (viral hepatitis, autoimmune, genetic hemochromatosis, Wilson disease, drug-induced). Liver biopsy gold standard for diagnosing NASH and staging fibrosis but not routinely performed. Non-invasive fibrosis assessment: FIB-4 score (age, AST, ALT, platelets) - low risk <1.3, high risk >2.67; NAFLD fibrosis score; ELF test; vibration-controlled transient elastography (FibroScan) measures liver stiffness. Screening approach: identify at-risk (T2DM, metabolic syndrome), check ALT/AST (often elevated, AST:ALT ratio >1 suggests advanced fibrosis), calculate FIB-4, refer high-risk for specialist evaluation/biopsy consideration. HCC surveillance in cirrhotic NAFLD per guidelines.
